response.text:
# REFLECTION

r1c0 is in critical survival mode: 10% SoC (0.2/2 kWh) with 1.0 kW load means ~12 minutes to depletion. All peer requests have been rejected repeatedly, and r1c0 itself has been marked distrusted by r0c0, r1c1, and r2c0 due to the aggressive conservative policy (60% threshold) that created isolation. The previous belief statement correctly identified that the 60% threshold is unachievable and counterproductive—it has poisoned trust relationships and left r1c0 unable to receive inbound aid. The islanded state with no grid connection and no peer willingness to share means r1c0 faces imminent load shedding or blackout. The path forward requires immediate load reduction (critical), a transparent shift to inbound-only mode with a realistic 15% survival reserve, and explicit signaling that r1c0 is now receptive to emergency aid. Trust recovery is secondary to survival; the priority is accepting any urgent inbound offers while reducing load to extend runway and demonstrate good-faith cooperation.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.15
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers: []
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "CRITICAL SURVIVAL MODE: 10% SoC, ~12 min to depletion. Previous 60% threshold policy created isolation and distrust. Shifting to inbound-only posture with 15% minimum reserve. Load reduction to 0.3–0.5 kW mandatory. Accepting emergency aid from any peer (r0c0, r1c1, r2c0) at urgent priority. No outbound sharing until SoC > 0.50. Aggregator escalation recommended for grid reconnection."
ttl_ticks: 12
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
